---
title: "T Cell Medium (RPMI + 10% FCS + Supplements)"
subtitle: "Abdullah Lab, IMMEI, University Hospital Bonn"
protocol_id: "BUF-TCELL-MED"
version: "v1.0"
date: "2026-03-02"
description: "Complete RPMI 1640 medium supplemented with FCS, Penicillin/Streptomycin, beta-mercaptoethanol, L-glutamine, and sodium pyruvate for mouse T cell and NLC culture."
---

**Protocol ID:** {< meta protocol_id >}  
**Version:** {< meta version >}  
**Author:** Dillon Corvino  

# Purpose

Complete T cell medium based on RPMI 1640, supplemented for optimal survival, activation, and expansion of mouse T cells and NLCs in vitro.

\tableofcontents

# Linked protocols

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)

this_buffer <- rmarkdown::metadata$protocol_id
deps <- read_csv(here::here("protocols_src/_dependencies.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

linked <- deps |>
  filter(buffer_id == this_buffer) |>
  distinct(protocol_id)

if (nrow(linked) == 0) {
  cat("None\n")
} else {
  linked |>
    mutate(line = glue::glue("- `{protocol_id}`")) |>
    pull(line) |>
    cat(sep = "\n")
}
```

---

# Working buffer composition

| Component                 | Final concentration | Notes                          |
|---------------------------|--------------------|--------------------------------|
| RPMI 1640                 | —                  | Base medium                   |
| Fetal calf serum (FCS)    | 10% (v/v)          | Heat-inactivated              |
| Penicillin/Streptomycin   | 1% (v/v)           | 100× stock → 1× final         |
| L-glutamine               | 2 mM               | From 200 mM stock             |
| Sodium pyruvate           | 1 mM               | From 100 mM stock             |
| beta-mercaptoethanol      | 50 µM              | From 50 mM stock              |

# Preparation

## Stock solutions required

| Reagent                    | Stock concentration | Dilution to final |
|----------------------------|--------------------|------------------|
| Fetal calf serum (FCS)     | —                  | 10% (v/v)        |
| Penicillin/Streptomycin    | 100×               | 1:100            |
| L-glutamine                | 200 mM             | 1:100            |
| Sodium pyruvate            | 100 mM             | 1:100            |
| beta-mercaptoethanol       | 50 mM              | 1:1000           |
| RPMI 1640                  | —                  | Base medium      |

## Volume calculations

Dilution logic:

- 10% FCS → 1:10  
- 1% Pen/Strep (100×) → 1:100  
- 2 mM L-glut from 200 mM → 1:100  
- 1 mM sodium pyruvate from 100 mM → 1:100  
- 50 µM beta-mercaptoethanol from 50 mM → 1:1000  

## Preparation table

| Reagent        | 10 mL | 50 mL | 100 mL | 200 mL | 500 mL |
| -------------- | ----- | ----- | ------ | ------ | ------ |
| RPMI (mL)      | 8.69  | 43.45 | 86.9   | 173.8  | 434.5  |
| FCS (mL)       | 1.0   | 5.0   | 10.0   | 20.0   | 50.0   |
| Pen/Strep (ul) | 100   | 500   | 1,000    | 2,000   | 5,000    |
| L-glut (uL)    | 100   | 500   | 1,000    | 2,000   | 5,000    |
| Pyruvate (uL)  | 100   | 500   | 1,000    | 2,000   | 5,000    |
| beta-ME (uL)   | 10  | 50  | 100    | 200    | 500    |

Label with:
   - *T Cell Complete Medium*  
   - Date of preparation  
   - Expiry (4 weeks after preparation)

::: callout-tip
Add beta-mercaptoethanol fresh where possible for optimal T cell viability.
:::

# Storage and stability

- Store at **4 °C**, protected from light.  
- Use within **4 weeks** after preparation.  
- Do not freeze.

# Reagent details

| Reagent                 | Supplier | Cat. # | Notes |
|-------------------------|----------|--------|-------|
| RPMI 1640               | Various  | —      | Base medium |
| Fetal calf serum        | Various  | —      | Heat-inactivated |
| Pen/Strep (100×)        | Various  | —      | Antibiotics |
| L-glutamine (200 mM)    | Various  | —      | Stable stock |
| Sodium pyruvate (100 mM)| Various  | —      | Optional supplement |
| beta-mercaptoethanol    | Various  | —      | Handle in hood |

# Safety

- Handle medium aseptically in a biosafety cabinet.  
- beta-mercaptoethanol is toxic and volatile; handle in a chemical hood.  
- Dispose of expired or contaminated medium as biohazardous waste.

# Version history

| Version | Date       | Author         | Changes |
|--------|------------|----------------|---------|
| v1.0   | 2026-03-02 | Dillon Corvino | Initial T cell medium document. |
